questions.question: Should requirements clarification be a mandatory gated step before spec authoring, or a recommended prompt that can be skipped for well-scoped requests?
questions.recommendation: Make it a recommended prompt (not a hard gate) — human can skip if the request is already well-scoped
questions.rationale: A hard gate adds friction for straightforward requests; a prompt covers the gap without blocking fast paths

questions.question: Should branch staleness block deliver --create-pr or only surface as a warning?
questions.recommendation: Warn, don't block — the human should decide whether to rebase first
questions.rationale: Blocking would add friction in cases where the staleness is intentional or harmless; a clear warning is sufficient

questions.question: Should verify_reconciliation.py be fixed to allow partial reconcile calls, or should reconcile always require all reviews to be passed?
questions.recommendation: Fix verify_reconciliation.py to check only that supplied reviews are present, not that no other entries exist
questions.rationale: Requiring all reviews defeats the purpose of the reconcile command in multi-stage workflows where reviews accumulate incrementally

questions.question: Should I-1 through I-4 ship as one wave or separately?
questions.recommendation: One wave — all four are low-cost docs/small-code changes with no interdependencies
questions.rationale: Batching reduces ceremony overhead; none of the four items require sequential dependency on each other

discovery.summary: Feature workflow has two completed improvement cycles (workflow-two-mode-implementation, workflow-runner-hardening). Six improvements tracked in feature-workflow-plan.md. I-1 through I-4 are strong candidates for a single low-cost wave: upfront clarification prompt, branch staleness warning, source-of-truth docs, and verify_reconciliation orphan fix. I-5 (runner self-containment) is a separate wave. I-6 (lightweight mode) is deferred.
